attributes.description:
Indicates whether or not the alert is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the alert, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the alert's open state.
attributes.name: open

attributes.description: Enables a close button that allows the user to dismiss the alert.
attributes.name: closable

attributes.description:
The length of time, in milliseconds, the alert will show before closing itself. If the user interacts with
the alert before it closes (e.g. moves the mouse over it), the timer will restart. Defaults to `Infinity`, meaning
the alert will not close on its own.
attributes.name: duration

cssParts.description: The container that wraps the optional icon.
cssParts.name: icon

cssParts.description: The container that wraps the alert's main content.
cssParts.name: message

events.description: Emitted after the alert opens and all animations are complete.
events.name: syn-after-show

events.description: Emitted after the alert closes and all animations are complete.
events.name: syn-after-hide

methods.description:
Displays the alert as a toast notification. This will move the alert out of its position in the DOM and, when
dismissed, it will be removed from the DOM completely. By storing a reference to the alert, you can reuse it by
calling this method again. The returned promise will resolve after the alert is hidden.
methods.name: toast

properties.description:
Indicates whether or not the alert is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the alert, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the alert's open state.
properties.name: open

properties.description: Enables a close button that allows the user to dismiss the alert.
properties.name: closable

properties.description:
The length of time, in milliseconds, the alert will show before closing itself. If the user interacts with
the alert before it closes (e.g. moves the mouse over it), the timer will restart. Defaults to `Infinity`, meaning
the alert will not close on its own.
properties.name: duration

slots.description: An icon to show in the alert. Works best with `<syn-icon>`.
slots.name: icon

summary: Alerts are used to display important messages inline or as toast notifications.
